coreJobStatement: Cultivate finfish from egg or fingerling to market-size harvest in a controlled aquatic environment
hierarchy: Process VN (L7 through L4) — what process steps and equipment compose the finfish production chain
marketSize: ~$5.2B globally (integrated RAS, 2024); projected $13B by 2034 at 9.6% CAGR; ~$472M filtration equipment [SRC: web_search]

l6Systems.name: Water Intake & Conditioning
l6Systems.jobFamily: Source, condition, and deliver water at required quality for rearing

l6Systems.name: Mechanical Filtration
l6Systems.jobFamily: Remove particulate solids (feces, uneaten feed) from rearing water

l6Systems.name: Biological Filtration
l6Systems.jobFamily: Convert toxic ammonia and nitrite to less-toxic nitrate via nitrifying bacteria

l6Systems.name: Gas Management
l6Systems.jobFamily: Strip CO2, inject dissolved oxygen to maintain respirable water

l6Systems.name: Disinfection
l6Systems.jobFamily: Kill pathogens (bacteria, viruses, parasites) in recirculating water

l6Systems.name: Temperature Control
l6Systems.jobFamily: Maintain species-specific water temperature for optimal growth

l6Systems.name: Fish Rearing
l6Systems.jobFamily: House fish from fingerling to harvest weight in controlled tanks

l6Systems.name: Feed Delivery
l6Systems.jobFamily: Distribute formulated feed to fish at optimized rates and schedules

l6Systems.name: Harvest & Grading
l6Systems.jobFamily: Remove market-size fish from rearing units, sort by size and quality

l6Systems.name: Water Quality Monitoring & Control (H)
l6Systems.jobFamily: Continuously sense DO, pH, ammonia, temperature, flow across all steps. MARQUARDT PRIMARY POSITION.

l6Systems.name: Biosecurity & Fish Health (H)
l6Systems.jobFamily: Quarantine, vaccinate, and monitor disease across all rearing units

l6Systems.name: Waste & Effluent Management (H)
l6Systems.jobFamily: Treat and discharge or reuse waste streams from filtration and harvest

l6Systems.name: Facility Utilities & Power (H)
l6Systems.jobFamily: Provide electrical power, emergency backup, compressed air, and building services

vnUnits.name: Source Water Pump Station
vnUnits.functionalJob: Lifts and delivers raw water from well, municipal supply, or surface source to the conditioning stage.

vnUnits.name: Pre-Filtration
vnUnits.functionalJob: Removes suspended sediment and large particulates before water enters the rearing loop.

vnUnits.name: Water Chemistry Adjustment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Conditions intake water to species-specific requirements for pH, alkalinity, and hardness.

vnUnits.name: Drum Filter
vnUnits.functionalJob: Dominant mechanical filtration technology in commercial RAS. Rotating drum screen with pressurized spray backwash. Screen mesh 40–100 μm. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Radial Flow Settler
vnUnits.functionalJob: Gravity-based particle separation for heavier settleable solids. Often used as primary removal ahead of drum filter.

vnUnits.name: Protein Skimmer / Foam Fractionator
vnUnits.functionalJob: Removes dissolved organic compounds and fine suspended particles via foam flotation. Critical for marine and high-density freshwater systems.

vnUnits.name: Moving Bed Biofilm Reactor (MBBR)
vnUnits.functionalJob: Dominant biofilter type in modern RAS. Neutrally buoyant plastic media (Kaldnes K1, BioElements) suspended by aeration. Specific surface area 500–800 m²/m³. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Trickling Tower / Biotower
vnUnits.functionalJob: Passive-aeration biofilter. Water trickles over structured media while air flows counter-current. Also serves as CO2 stripper. Common in smaller RAS. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Fluidized Sand Biofilter
vnUnits.functionalJob: High-efficiency biofilter using fine sand (0.2–0.5 mm) as media. Superior nitrification rate per volume but higher energy cost.

vnUnits.name: Oxygen Supply
vnUnits.functionalJob: Pure oxygen delivery to the facility. On-site generation (PSA/VPSA) for large farms; liquid oxygen (LOX) for smaller or backup.

vnUnits.name: Oxygen Injection
vnUnits.functionalJob: Dissolves pure oxygen into rearing water at high transfer efficiency.

vnUnits.name: CO2 Stripper / Degasser
vnUnits.functionalJob: Removes dissolved carbon dioxide from recirculating water. Often combined with trickling tower biofilter.

vnUnits.name: UV Sterilizer
vnUnits.functionalJob: UV-C irradiation destroying bacteria, viruses, and parasites. Sized by UV dose (40–100 mJ/cm² depending on target pathogen). Standard in nearly all RAS.

vnUnits.name: Ozone Treatment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Ozone (O3) injection for advanced disinfection and micro-flocculation. Requires careful dosing — excess ozone is toxic to fish. [SRC: web_search]

vnUnits.name: Heat Exchanger
vnUnits.functionalJob: Transfers thermal energy between rearing water and the heating/cooling source without mixing fluids. Plate or shell-and-tube designs.

vnUnits.name: Chiller / Heat Pump
vnUnits.functionalJob: Active cooling for coldwater species or tropical facility climate control. Scroll or screw compressor with R-410A or R-134a refrigerant.

vnUnits.name: Heating System
vnUnits.functionalJob: Heats water for warmwater species or maintains hatchery spawning temperature. Boiler, electric heater, or heat pump in reverse mode.

vnUnits.name: Grow-Out Tank
vnUnits.functionalJob: Primary rearing vessel for market-size production. Circular tanks (6–15 m diameter) in RAS; rectangular raceways in flow-through systems. Stocking density 40–100 kg/m³ for RAS salmonids.

vnUnits.name: Fry / Fingerling Tank
vnUnits.functionalJob: Smaller nursery tanks for juvenile stages (1–50 g). Require gentler water currents and finer mesh screens to prevent escape.

vnUnits.name: Tank Hydrodynamics
vnUnits.functionalJob: Systems creating self-cleaning water flow patterns that sweep solids to center drain while distributing oxygen uniformly.

vnUnits.name: Automatic Feeder
vnUnits.functionalJob: Delivers pelletized feed to tanks on programmable schedules. Pneumatic, belt, or screw feeder types.

vnUnits.name: Feed Storage & Handling
vnUnits.functionalJob: Bulk storage and distribution of formulated feed from delivery to feeder hopper.

vnUnits.name: Feed Monitoring
vnUnits.functionalJob: Detects uneaten feed and optimizes feeding regime for feed conversion efficiency.

vnUnits.name: Grading Machine
vnUnits.functionalJob: Sorts fish by size for uniform batches (market requirement) and splits undersize fish back to grow-out.

vnUnits.name: Fish Pump / Transfer
vnUnits.functionalJob: Moves live fish from rearing tanks to grading or slaughter with minimal stress and scale damage.

vnUnits.name: Stunning & Slaughter
vnUnits.functionalJob: Humane killing per animal welfare standards (electrical, percussive, or CO2 stunning followed by bleeding).

vnUnits.name: Flow Measurement
vnUnits.functionalJob: Measures water circulation rate in main loop, individual tank supply, and treatment stages. Flow failure alarm is the most critical safety trigger in RAS.

vnUnits.name: Dissolved Gas Sensing
vnUnits.functionalJob: Monitors dissolved oxygen (most critical parameter), CO2, and total dissolved gas pressure.

vnUnits.name: Chemical Parameter Sensing
vnUnits.functionalJob: Monitors ammonia, pH, nitrite, and turbidity — the core water chemistry parameters for fish health.

vnUnits.name: Central SCADA / Control
vnUnits.functionalJob: Aggregates all sensor data, runs control algorithms, and generates alarms for out-of-range conditions.

vnUnits.name: Quarantine System
vnUnits.functionalJob: Isolated water circuit for incoming fish, preventing pathogen introduction to main production.

vnUnits.name: Vaccination & Treatment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Preventive and therapeutic interventions for disease control.

vnUnits.name: Fish Surveillance
vnUnits.functionalJob: Continuous monitoring of fish behavior, mortality, and health indicators for early disease detection.

vnUnits.name: Sludge Handling
vnUnits.functionalJob: Collects, concentrates, and removes solid waste from drum filter backwash and settler drains.

vnUnits.name: Effluent Treatment
vnUnits.functionalJob: Treats liquid discharge to meet environmental permit requirements before release.

vnUnits.name: Nutrient Recovery
vnUnits.functionalJob: Converts waste nutrients into value-added by-products (fertilizer, compost, aquaponics crops).

vnUnits.name: Emergency Power
vnUnits.functionalJob: Backup electrical supply ensuring water circulation and oxygenation continue during grid failure.

vnUnits.name: Compressed Air / Blower System
vnUnits.functionalJob: Provides air for aeration, MBBR agitation, pneumatic valve actuation, and ozone generation.

vnUnits.name: Building Services
vnUnits.functionalJob: Facility-level infrastructure supporting the production environment.
